indicator_family_glossary.definition: Tracks price relative to active price-sentiment overlap bands and alert/event states.
indicator_family_glossary.interpretation_notes: 50 neutral; confirmed/watch events move score away from 50; fades with recency.

indicator_family_glossary.definition: Sentiment-enhanced MACD model combining classical price MACD and sentiment MACD relationship logic.
indicator_family_glossary.interpretation_notes: Raw price MACD stays public-comparable; relationship scores are SETA-specific.

indicator_family_glossary.definition: State/timing model using price RSI, sentiment RSI, their relationship and stochastic timing.
indicator_family_glossary.interpretation_notes: Separates direction/repair/exhaustion rather than one simplistic RSI score.

indicator_family_glossary.definition: Sentiment moving-average stack, slope and structure model.
indicator_family_glossary.interpretation_notes: Splits direction from structure so a ribbon can be bearish but clean, or bullish but unstable.

indicator_family_glossary.definition: Trend structure from price and sentiment moving average ladders.
indicator_family_glossary.interpretation_notes: Above 50 bullish stacking; below 50 bearish/mixed stacking.
Confirms or discounts directional setups without replacing pure signal scores.

indicator_family_glossary.definition: Combines families into direction, confidence, dispersion, archetypes and priority.
indicator_family_glossary.interpretation_notes: Priority is review urgency, not bullishness.

score_glossary.definition: A neutral-centered directional score where 50 is neutral/mixed, >50 bullish/positive, <50 bearish/negative.
score_glossary.where_used: Used for Bollinger, MACD, RSI, ribbon, MA trend and consensus families.
score_glossary.caution: Do not treat higher as always better without checking direction context.

score_glossary.definition: A centered score where 0 is neutral, positive is bullish, negative is bearish.
score_glossary.where_used: Used when calculating consensus from family deviations from neutral.
score_glossary.caution: Range may be approximate depending on available inputs.

score_glossary.definition: A triage score for review urgency, not directional bullishness.
score_glossary.where_used: Used to rank assets in the screener.

score_glossary.definition: A score for reliability/clarity of a signal family or archetype.
score_glossary.where_used: Used to separate clean from noisy setups.
score_glossary.caution: High confidence does not guarantee outcome.

score_glossary.definition: A disagreement measure across signal families.
score_glossary.where_used: Low dispersion means families agree; high dispersion means conflict.
score_glossary.caution: High priority plus high dispersion may still be worth review, but needs caution.

score_glossary.definition: A multiplier that fades event-driven pressure back toward neutral over time.
score_glossary.where_used: Used in Bollinger and crossover scores.
score_glossary.caution: A stale event can remain visible but should not dominate.

score_glossary.definition: Incremental pressure from repeated recent watch candidates.
score_glossary.caution: Watch clusters are developmental, not confirmed.

score_glossary.definition: A standardized relationship measure used only for SETA relationship scoring.
score_glossary.where_used: Used for sentiment-price spread normalization in MACD/RSI relationship logic.
score_glossary.caution: Not intended to replace public comparable raw price indicators.

score_glossary.definition: Difference between sentiment and price versions of an indicator after appropriate scaling/normalization.
score_glossary.where_used: Used to capture sentiment leading/lagging price.
score_glossary.caution: Interpret with slope and crossover for best context.

score_glossary.definition: Change in an indicator or spread over time.
score_glossary.where_used: Used to capture improvement/deterioration and joint slope labels.
score_glossary.caution: Can be noisy on short windows.

score_glossary.definition: Neutral-centered event score for a fresh bullish/bearish crossover.
score_glossary.where_used: Used for sentiment MACD signal crossing price MACD signal.

score_glossary.definition: Rule-based setup classification derived from combinations of indicator states.
score_glossary.where_used: Used for readable summaries and risk notes.
score_glossary.caution: Archetypes are descriptive diagnostics, not trade instructions.

column_descriptions.description: Rank ordering of assets by review priority.
